title: wrightia arborea
intro: Wrightia arborea, also known as the Indian cork tree or Milk bush, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Its leaves, bark, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their diverse medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in managing diabetes and digestive problems. Wrightia arborea can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
